textPlain: While the author doesn't seem to like version based APIs very much, I always recommend baking them in from the very start of your application.You cannot predict the future and chances are there will be some breaking change forced upon you by someone or something out of your control.
We do not break userspace> Mauro, SHUT THE FUCK UP!
Are there good public examples of well designed APIs that have stood the test of time?
> You should let people use your APIs with a long-lived API key.Sigh... I wish this were not true. It's a shame that no alternatives have emerged so far.

I always thought the Amiga APIs with the tag lists were cool. You easily could extend the API/ABI w/o breaking anything at the binary level (assuming you made the calls accept tag lists as parameters to begin with, of course).
